scenario_summary: One host runs the stable channel and the preview channel at the same time. Each channel keeps its own configuration root, recovery root, derived cache root, keychain entries, update marker, recent-item registration, file-association candidacy, and protocol-handler scheme. No cross-channel state merge is implied; both channels remain claim-bearing on their own install-profile cards.

explanation_fields.user_facing_summary: Stable and preview channels run side by side on this host. Each channel keeps its own settings, layout, recovery state, file associations, and deep-link handlers; nothing is merged across channels.
explanation_fields.release_notes_summary: Side-by-side coexistence with preview is supported. Each channel keeps its own state roots, update marker, recent-item list, and protocol-handler scheme. The shared aureline:// scheme resolves to whichever channel the user or admin selected as the default.
explanation_fields.support_summary: Support exports stay in the exact-build support family for whichever channel produced the bundle; no cross-channel redaction rewrite is required because no state is shared.
explanation_fields.promotion_summary: Promote-eligible. The release packet cites one install-profile card per channel and both rows resolve through the pair.stable_and_preview admission row without narrowing claims.

notes: Default smoke posture for stable + preview side-by-side review.
